1The Governmen t of the Feder al Democratic Republic 
of Ethiopia (FDRE) recently embark ed on reforming 
the Public Financial Managemen t (PFM) systems by 
implemen ting the Integrated Financial Managemen t 
Informa tion S ystem (IFMIS). 
The IFMIS is a budge t and expenditur e managemen t 
system that will enable the gener ation of accurat e, 
informa tion and reports, support public bodies and 
regions in the developmen t of better macr oeconomic of servic e in accor danc e with agreed priorities, and in 
the process, contribut e to the alleviation of poverty in 
Ethiopia. 
Amongst other things, it also provides necessary 
platform and the tool set to undertak e key PFM reform 
initia tives like Program Based Budge ting, Accrual 
other systems such as Banks and ERCA, having single 

2OBJECTIVES OF IFMIS IMPLEMENTATION
• To form standard process across the Public Bodies 
from Budgeting till Reporting as per the government 
policy and procedure 
• Aims to support public bodies and regions to generate 
accurate, accessible and timely government-wide 
/f_inancial information 
• Leverages the advantage of systems where data is 
entered only once but used/referenced and Reported 
every where 
• To establish single source of information to assist 
decision making in Public Finance matters 
• Provides the information to ensure improved 
eﬃciency and eﬀectiveness of government /f_inancial 
management 
INTEGRATED FINANCIAL MANAGEMENT INFORMATION 
SYSTEM (IFMIS) ETHIOPIA
• Increases availability of comprehensive /f_inancial 
information on current and past performance, assists 
budgetary control and improved economic forecasting, 
planning, and budgeting 
• Improve the lengthy service delivery by using 
technology as an enabler to have eﬃcient and 
eﬀective service to the citizens; 
• Availing real time information to decision makers from 
one source and via integration from other vertical 
systems like Banks, Revenue sources and other 
stakeholders 
• Improve the current limitation of /f_inancial system and 
lack of system support for property administration, 
/f_ixed asset management, procurement and payroll

3Techno Brain Consortium was contracted in 2016 to /f_ix gaps and enhance the IFMIS system along with upgrdation of 
hardware followed by rollout IFMIS across 156 federal sites. As part of the rollout project  scope, the Consortium in 
coordination with Ministry of Finance has been providing capacity building to the  implementation of public bodies as 
well as designing a User Manual in an eﬀort to streamline the Public Financial Management reform to the end user level.  
Techno Brain Consortium has completed the rollout of IFMIS to 156 public bodies post completion of system 
enhancement and Infrastructure Upgrade to the latest and /f_irst in Ethiopia, Oracle Supercluster M7 server technology 
as well as providing CISCO Network Equipments and end user desktop machines as a turnkey project.  All users have 
been trained and system is now under one year support and maintenance mode. 

1. Rolling out IFMIS (9 modules) across 156 sites - Supply, sizing and installation of data center hardware including 
related training, support, and maintenance.
2. Fix 6 gaps identi/f_ied by MoF on existing modules implemented before rollout.
 
3. Supply and install all computing hardware – Oracle Super Clusters, Oracle Sun Storage units switches, Routers, 
Firewall, etc.
 
4. Data collection, conversion, cleansing, porting and migration at the 156 Federal IFMIS Sites.
 
5. Training of 9,500 approximately end-users and capacity building/Knowledge Transfer to relevant and selected 
staﬀ including the provision of the training material.
 
6. Creating and Updating all necessary user documentation. 
7. Successful completion of Operational Acceptance Testing / User Acceptance Testing at the 156 Federal sites.
 
8. Provision of a minimum of 1 (one) month on-site support after the completion of  operational acceptance at the 
156 Federal Sites.
 
9. Project Management oﬃce through its Quality Assurance Service to ensure successful  implementation of  the Project.
10. Provision of post-implementation support as per the agreed Service Level Agreement on a yearly basis.

users (From Top Managemen t to End User] of the public bodies. All the modules of IFMIS has been categoriz ed based

Trained 9,500 end-users under capacity building/Knowledge Transfer to relevant and selected staﬀ including the

roles such as Financial, Procurement, Planning (Budget), Auditor, Human Resource, Payroll, Inventory, Fixed Asset, 
Petty Cashier, Property Administration, Self Service and Top Management. 
Moreover , capacity building activities have been done on areas of project managemen t, change managemen t,
